title: NOC 14403 – Purchasing and Inventory Control Workers | GTR Immigration
description: Purchasing and Inventory Control Workers play a critical role in ensuring smooth supply-chain operations by managing procurement, tracking stock levels, and coordinating replenishment across retail, manufacturing, and government environments in Canada. Classified under NOC 14403, this occupation has experienced strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—creating practical immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Expansion of domestic manufacturingGrowth in e-commerce and retail logisticsEntry-level TEER occupation with supervisory pathwaysEmployer-supported recruitmentInventory-automation initiatives
classification.major_group: 144 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations
classification.minor_group: 1440 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations

duties: Review requisitions and verify stock availability
Request supplier quotations and prepare purchase orders
Calculate order costs and forward invoices
Coordinate delivery schedules and resolve supply issues
Maintain purchasing records and price lists
Track stock movements using computerized systems
Manage stock rotation and obsolete inventory
Reconcile physical counts with system records
employment_requirements: Courses in purchasing management (often required for procurement roles)
Training in production or inventory systems
Ability to operate computerized inventory software
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 6,200 new positions for Purchasing and Inventory Control Workers, while around 5,900 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Purchasing and Inventory Control Workers are in demand across manufacturing, retail, wholesale, and public-sector operations nationwide. Hiring has expanded rapidly since the pandemic and is expected to continue over the coming years.
Retail and wholesale distribution centresManufacturing plantsGovernment procurement departmentsLogistics firmsIndustrial operations
Annual salaries typically range between $28,900 and $64,800 CAD, depending on province, experience, and technical expertise.
